title: A Bengal Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of good mutton
- one onion
- butter
- one clove of garlic
- one tablespoonful (or two if required very hot) of currie powder
- two ounces of butter
- good gravy
- tamarind juice or lemon

## Directions

1. Wash and cut up into pieces, about an inch square, two pounds or rather less of good mutton
2. Put the pieces of meat into a stewpan
3. Add an onion previously fried in butter, and a clove of garlic, chopped fine
4. Sprinkle over the meat one tablespoonful (or two if the currie is required to be very hot) of currie powder
5. Brown two ounces of butter in a frying-pan, and pour it over the meat
6. Add sufficient good gravy to cover it
7. Let it stew gently for two hours
8. Add some tamarind juice, or lemon to give the required acid
9. Thicken the gravy and serve

title: A Madras Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- one fowl
- butter
- two tablespoonfuls of currie powder
- one lemon
- half a cocoa nut
- one teaspoonful of salt
- fried onion
- one clove of garlic
- butter and flour

## Directions

1. Skin a fowl and cut it up into small joints
2. Fry it in butter a light brown
3. Put it into a saucepan with two tablespoonfuls of currie powder, the juice of a lemon, half a cocoa nut finely grated, a teaspoonful of salt, a little fried onion, and a clove of garlic
4. Let it simmer slowly for three hours, adding a thickening of butter and flour, just before it is sufficiently cooked

title: A Relish
body:
## Ingredients

- cayenne
- ground ginger
- black pepper
- cinnamon
- mace
- white pepper
- grated nutmegs
- ground cloves
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take of cayenne, ground ginger, black pepper, cinnamon and mace each one quarter of an ounce
2. of white pepper and grated nutmegs one ounce, and half a teaspoonful of ground cloves
3. mix well and put into a bottle
4. it will be found a great addition to soups and gravies, and mixed with a little vinegar to form a paste will make a delicious sauce for cold meat, steaks, &c.

title: Carrack
body:
## Ingredients

- eight pickled walnuts
- one head of garlic
- walnut vinegar
- soy
- mushroom catsup
- a tablespoonful of Harvey sauce
- one quart of vinegar
- mango pickle

## Directions

1. Chop eight pickled walnuts and one head of garlic, put these into a large jar
2. add walnut vinegar, soy, and mushroom catsup, of each half a tumblerful, a tablespoonful of Harvey sauce, and one quart of vinegar
3. put the jar in a dry place, and shake it every day for a month
4. a few spoonfuls of mango pickle is a great improvement

title: Cayenne Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- half an ounce of Cayenne pepper
- a pint of white wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Put half an ounce of Cayenne pepper into a bottle with a pint of white wine vinegar, cork it tightly, and shake it well for a few days
2. it will be soon ready for use

title: Chili Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- four dozen fresh chilies
- a pint of strong vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop, and pound in a mortar four dozen fresh chilies, and put them into a bottle with a pint of strong vinegar
2. shake the bottle every day, and in three weeks it will be ready for use

title: Chutney, No. 1
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound or a little more of raisins stoned and cut in halves
- two onions
- one garlic
- one eschalot
- about twenty chilies well pounded in a mortar
- half a pound of mustard
- a quarter of a pound of fine ginger
- a quarter of a pound of salt or not quite as much
- one pound of sour apples
- a pint of vinegar
- one pound of brown sugar previously boiled to a syrup, or the same quantity of treacle or golden syrup
- about three pints of vinegar

## Directions

1. Put into a large basin or pan, half a pound, or a little more, of raisins stoned and cut in halves, two onions, one garlic, one eschalot, and about twenty chilies well pounded in a mortar
2. add half a pound of mustard, a quarter of a pound of fine ginger, and a quarter of a pound of salt, or not quite as much
3. peel and slice one pound of sour apples, put them in a jar with a pint of vinegar
4. cover the jar well, and put it into the oven and bake until the apples are rather soft
5. When they are cold, add them to the other ingredients with one pound of brown sugar previously boiled to a syrup, or the same quantity of treacle or golden syrup, and about three pints of vinegar, including the quantity the apples were baked in
6. Stir all the ingredients well together, and bottle for use

title: Chutney, No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of pudding raisins
- a quarter of a pound of apples
- the same quantity of moist sugar
- chopped tomatos from which you have taken the seeds
- two ounces of tamarinds
- a tablespoonful of salt
- a tablespoonful of cayenne
- a teaspoonful of ground ginger
- two large onions
- very little chopped lemon peel
- a pint of lemon juice
- one quart of vinegar

## Directions

1. Stone and cut up into rather large pieces, a quarter of a pound of pudding raisins
2. add to these, a quarter of a pound of apples chopped in small pieces, the same quantity of moist sugar, and chopped tomatos from which you have taken the seeds, two ounces of tamarinds, a tablespoonful of salt, ditto of cayenne, a teaspoonful of ground ginger, two large onions chopped fine, very little chopped lemon peel, a pint of lemon juice, and one quart of vinegar
3. mix all these ingredients well together
4. then put them into a jar, well covered
5. Stir the mixture every day for four or five weeks
6. if at the end of that time it is too thin, pour enough of the liquid away to make it of a proper thickness, but not dry
7. bottle the chutney in bottles or jars for use

title: Cocoa Nut Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarters of a pound grated cocoa nut
- three quarters of a pound pounded loaf sugar
- one teaspoonful arrow root
- one egg

## Directions

1. Mix three quarters of a pound of grated cocoa nut, the same quantity of pounded loaf sugar, and one teaspoonful of arrow root with one egg
2. Bake on buttered papers

title: Cocoa Nut Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh cocoa nut
- sweet almonds
- bitter almond
- two or three ounces sifted white sugar
- white of egg

## Directions

1. Grate a fresh cocoa nut, and leave it to dry
2. Add to it a few sweet almonds and one bitter almond, also grated, two or three ounces of sifted white sugar, and sufficient well-beaten white of egg to make the whole into a stiff paste
3. Roll it into round balls
4. Bake on a greased tin until they are quite dry, and the top of them slightly browned
5. Put them off on to a cold dish

title: Cocoa Nut Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh cocoa nut
- one ounce candied citron
- half a lemon
- quarter of a pound sifted white sugar
- two ounces fresh butter
- yolks of three or four eggs
- paste

## Directions

1. Remove the dark rind from the cocoa nut and grate it very finely
2. Chop up an ounce of candied citron very small, and grate the rind of half a lemon
3. Put these ingredients into a basin, adding to them a quarter of a pound of sifted white sugar
4. Oil over the fire two ounces of fresh butter, and pour it over the other ingredients
5. Mix well, and put to them the yolks of three or four eggs
6. Add a squeeze of lemon, and pour into a tart dish lined with paste
7. Bake in a slow oven

title: Cocoa Nut Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces of finely-grated cocoa nut
- three pints of veal stock
- corn or rice flour
- salt
- half a pint of cream
- nutmeg or mace

## Directions

1. Put three ounces of finely-grated cocoa nut (omitting the dark rind) to three pints of veal stock
2. Boil it gently for an hour
3. Mix with a little cold stock enough corn or rice flour to make the soup sufficiently thick
4. Season to taste
5. Add half a pint of cream
6. Simmer all together for about a quarter of an hour
7. Nutmeg or mace may be added if liked

title: Currie Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four onions
- two ounces of butter
- flour
- a large tablespoonful of currie powder
- three tablespoonfuls of gravy soup
- a fowl
- a large tablespoonful of tamarinds
- rice

## Directions

1. Mince small three or four onions according to their size
2. Put them into a saucepan with two ounces of butter
3. Dredge in some flour and fry them of a light brown, taking care not to burn them
4. Rub in by degrees a large tablespoonful of currie powder till it is quite a paste
5. Gradually stir in three tablespoonfuls of gravy soup, mixing it well together
6. Boil it gently until it is well flavoured with the currie powder
7. Strain it into another saucepan and add a fowl skinned and cut into small pieces
8. Stew it slowly for an hour
9. Take out half a pint of the soup and stew in it a large tablespoonful of tamarinds until you can easily take away the stones
10. Strain and stir into the soup
11. Boil all together for a quarter of an hour
12. Serve rice with it

title: Curried Cod
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of cod
- half a fried onion
- good stock
- teaspoonful of currie powder
- two ounces of butter
- salt
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Take a piece of cod, pull it into large flakes, and fry it till brown
2. Put this into a stewpan, with half a fried onion
3. Pour over it sufficient good stock to cover it
4. Add a teaspoonful of currie powder, and two ounces of butter, with salt to taste; also the juice of half a lemon
5. Simmer for a quarter of an hour, or until the fish is cooked, thicken the gravy, and serve

title: Curried Sole
body:
## Ingredients

- a filleted sole
- vinegar
- gravy
- fried onions
- currie powder
- a large lump of butter, about two ounces

## Directions

1. Take a filleted sole, a large thick one, cut it into pieces, not too small, lay them in vinegar for an hour
2. Have ready some gravy, prepared with fried onions and currie powder, as for prawn currie
3. Add to it the pieces of sole, and a large lump of butter, about two ounces
4. Simmer gently for half an hour, or rather longer if the sole is thick

title: Curried Sweetbreads
body:
## Ingredients

- good veal gravy
- fried onion
- one tablespoonful of vinegar or lemon juice
- one tablespoonful of currie powder
- salt
- two ounces of butter
- flour
- two or three sweetbreads

## Directions

1. Have ready some good veal gravy, add to it a very small quantity of fried onion, a tablespoonful of vinegar or the juice of a lemon, the same quantity of currie powder, and salt to taste
2. Rub two ounces of butter into enough flour to make this gravy (which ought to be about three-quarters of a pint) a proper thickness
3. Cut up two or three sweetbreads into pieces about two inches square
4. Stew them gently in the gravy until sufficiently cooked, and serve

title: Curried Sweetbreads
body:
## Ingredients

- veal gravy
- fried onion
- tablespoonful of vinegar or lemon juice
- tablespoonful of currie powder
- salt
- two ounces of butter
- flour
- two or three sweetbreads

## Directions

1. Have ready some good veal gravy, add to it a very small quantity of fried onion, a tablespoonful of vinegar or the juice of a lemon, the same quantity of currie powder, and salt to taste
2. Rub two ounces of butter into enough flour to make the gravy (which ought to be about three-quarters of a pint) a proper thickness
3. Cut up two or three sweetbreads into pieces about two inches square
4. Stew them gently in the gravy until sufficiently cooked, and serve

title: Devil Pot
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarts of the best vinegar
- eight ounces of salt
- two ounces of ginger
- half an ounce of mace
- a quarter of a pound of eschalots
- one ounce of white pepper
- one ounce of mustard seed
- half a tablespoonful of good cayenne pepper
- fruits or vegetables

## Directions

1. To three quarts of the best vinegar, put eight ounces of salt, two ounces of ginger, half an ounce of mace, a quarter of a pound of eschalots, one ounce of white pepper, one ounce of mustard seed, and half a tablespoonful of good cayenne pepper
2. boil it all together and when cold put it into a jar
3. gather any fruits or vegetables you like, wipe them clean and put them into your pickle
4. secure the jar closely by tying leather and a bladder over it

title: Dry Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of butter
- one tablespoonful of currie powder
- stock
- fowl or uncooked meat
- onion
- three cloves
- a small piece of stick cinnamon
- three cardamom seeds
- two bay leaves

## Directions

1. Melt two ounces of butter in a frying pan until it is a little burnt
2. Mix with it a tablespoonful of currie powder, and let it fry till brown
3. Put it with very little stock into a saucepan
4. Cut up your fowl, or any uncooked meat, into pieces
5. Add a little onion cut very small, three cloves, a small piece of stick cinnamon, three cardamom seeds, and two bay leaves
6. Let all simmer together for two hours or longer, using very little stock so there should be no gravy when served up

title: English Guava Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- some very fine blackberries
- three quarters of a pound of loaf sugar (for every pint of juice)

## Directions

1. Take the stalks off some very fine blackberries
2. Boil them in a preserving pan until all the juice is extracted, pressing them with a wooden spoon
3. Strain through muslin
4. To every pint of juice add three quarters of a pound of loaf sugar
5. Boil for half an hour or longer, until it becomes a firm jelly, which you may know by dropping some of it on a plate to cool
6. It must be carefully skimmed whilst boiling

title: Eschalot Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart bottle nearly full of vinegar
- five ounces of eschalots
- half a teaspoonful of Cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Put into a quart bottle nearly full of vinegar, five ounces of eschalots which have been well bruised, and add half a teaspoonful of Cayenne pepper
2. cork the bottle well, shake it up, and then leave it for a fortnight
3. at the end of this time, strain it through fine muslin, and bottle it again

title: Hard Egg Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- two onions
- butter
- tablespoonful of currie powder
- pint of good stock
- cup of cream
- arrowroot or rice flour
- six or eight hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Slice two onions, and fry them in butter
2. Boil them with a tablespoonful of currie powder in a pint of good stock until quite cooked
3. Then add a cup of cream, and thicken with arrowroot or rice flour
4. Simmer it slowly for a few minutes, adding six or eight hard boiled eggs cut into slices
5. Heat the eggs thoroughly, but do not let them boil

title: Hot Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- two cloves of garlic
- a pint of vinegar
- a tablespoonful of Worcester sauce
- a teaspoonful of salt
- an ounce of ground white pepper
- half an ounce of cayenne

## Directions

1. Chop fine two cloves of garlic, put this into a bottle, with a pint of vinegar, a tablespoonful of Worcester sauce, a teaspoonful of salt, an ounce of ground white pepper, and half an ounce of cayenne
2. Shake it up well, then let it remain for a month
3. Strain it through fine muslin and bottle it in small bottles, which should be well corked down

title: Imitation Preserved Ginger
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound loaf sugar
- four pints water
- vegetable marrow

## Directions

1. Make a syrup with one pound of loaf sugar to four pints of water
2. Peel a vegetable marrow, remove the seeds and pulp, and cut it into pieces two or three inches in length
3. Pour the syrup over the vegetable marrow, and let it remain two or three days
4. Drain it from the syrup, and to each pound of marrow add the juice of one

title: Imitation Preserved Ginger
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of loaf sugar
- four pints of water
- one vegetable marrow
- the juice of one lemon
- the peel of half a lemon
- half an ounce (or rather more) of white Jamaica ginger (sliced)
- one pound of loaf sugar
- a quarter of a teaspoonful of cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Make a syrup with one pound of loaf sugar to four pints of water
2. Peel a vegetable marrow, remove the seeds and pulp, and cut it into pieces two or three inches in length
3. Pour the syrup over the vegetable marrow, and let it remain two or three days
4. Drain it from the syrup, and to each pound of marrow add the juice of one, and the peel of half a lemon, half an ounce, or rather more, of white Jamaica ginger (sliced), one pound of loaf sugar, and a quarter of a teaspoonful of cayenne pepper
5. Boil all this together until the marrow is tender and looks clear
6. The cayenne may be put into a muslin bag and so boiled, being removed after the preserve is cooked, if the pungency of the pepper be objected to

title: Indian Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflowers
- French beans
- peas
- gherkins
- small onions
- capsicums
- bird's-eye chilies
- garlic
- eschalots
- white hearts of summer cabbage
- tomato skins
- vinegar
- salt
- currie powder
- mustard seed

## Directions

1. Wash the vegetables with vinegar and dry them
2. Put them on large dishes, sprinkled over with salt, and left for twenty-four hours
3. Dry them carefully and put into a large jar
4. Boil as much vinegar as will be required to cover them, putting to each quart of vinegar one tablespoonful and a half of currie powder (or two if the currie powder is not very hot), and an ounce of mustard seed
5. Pour this vinegar boiling over the vegetables, and when cold tie the jar down, and leave it for twelve hours or longer
6. Turn the vegetables out of the jar into a large basin, and put them, free of liquid, into smaller bottles for use, taking care to place the tomato skins at the sides of the bottles
7. Boil the vinegar again, adding more and currie powder in proportion, if you have not enough to fill every bottle quite full, and pour it boiling hot over the vegetables in the bottles
8. When cold, tie down with bladders; it must be kept a year

title: J. P.'s Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- one fowl
- two ounces of butter
- three sliced onions
- one pint of boiling stock
- one tablespoonful of currie powder
- flour
- cold water
- two sour apples
- half a lemon

## Directions

1. Cut up a fowl into small joints and put it into a stewpan with two ounces of butter, and three sliced onions
2. Let it stew until the meat has acquired a nice brown colour, but do not allow it to get black
3. Add one pint of boiling stock
4. Mix one tablespoonful of currie powder and the same quantity of flour, with a little cold water into a smooth paste, and put this into the stewpan
5. Peel and slice two sour apples (taking out the core), which add to the fowl, and other ingredients
6. Let all stew gently, for at least an hour
7. Squeeze into it the juice of half a lemon and serve

title: Kebobbed Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- onions
- uncooked veal
- currie powder
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut up some apples and onions into slices, and some uncooked veal into round slices the same size
2. Have ready some small skewers and put upon each skewer twelve slices of meat, apples, and onions, alternately
3. Sprinkle well over them some currie powder
4. Fry them in a stewpan, with sufficient butter to cover them
5. Send to table without removing the skewers

title: Lobster Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- two onions
- half an ounce of butter
- one tablespoonful and a half of currie powder
- half a pint of good gravy
- meat from a large lobster
- tablespoonful of vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Fry two onions in half an ounce of butter, until they are nicely browned
2. Mix one tablespoonful and a half of currie powder, with half a pint of good gravy, and put this with the fried onions into a stewpan
3. Take the meat from a large lobster, cut it into rather small pieces, and add it to the gravy and onions with a tablespoonful of vinegar or lemon juice
4. Simmer slowly for about half an hour, and serve

title: Lord Clive's Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- six sliced onions
- one green apple
- a clove of garlic
- a little good stock
- one teaspoonful of currie powder
- a few tablespoonfuls of stock
- a salt-spoonful of salt
- a salt-spoonful of Cayenne pepper
- uncooked meat
- a piece of butter

## Directions

1. Take six sliced onions, one green apple, and a clove of garlic
2. Stew them to a pulp in a little good stock
3. Add one teaspoonful of currie powder, a few tablespoonfuls of stock, a salt-spoonful of salt, and the same quantity of Cayenne pepper
4. Add to this gravy any kind of uncooked meat cut into small pieces
5. Add a piece of butter

title: Lord Clive's Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- six sliced onions
- one green apple
- one clove of garlic
- good stock
- one teaspoonful of currie powder
- a few tablespoonfuls of stock
- a salt-spoonful of salt
- Cayenne pepper
- uncooked meat
- a piece of butter the size of a walnut
- flour

## Directions

1. Stew the onions, apple, and garlic to a pulp in a little good stock
2. Add one teaspoonful of currie powder, a few tablespoonfuls of stock, a salt-spoonful of salt, and the same quantity of Cayenne pepper
3. Add to this gravy any kind of uncooked meat cut into small pieces, adding a piece of butter the size of a walnut, rolled in flour
4. Stew slowly for two hours and a half

title: Madras Anchovy Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of butter the size of an apple
- well-beaten yolks of two eggs
- cayenne pepper to taste
- half a teaspoonful of white wine or a teaspoonful of beer
- a tablespoonful of anchovy paste
- hot toast

## Directions

1. Put a piece of butter the size of an apple upon a very hot plate
2. add the well-beaten yolks of two eggs, cayenne pepper to taste, half a teaspoonful of white wine, or a teaspoonful of beer, and a tablespoonful of anchovy paste
3. Mix well
4. soak hot toast in it, and serve very hot

title: Malay Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of almonds
- butter
- one onion
- the rind of half a lemon
- one dessert-spoonful of currie powder
- half a pint of water
- one chicken
- one teacup-ful of cream
- one lemon

## Directions

1. Blanch two ounces of almonds
2. Fry them in a little butter until they are brown, but do not let them burn
3. Pound them to a cream with an onion and the rind of half a lemon
4. Mix a dessert-spoonful of currie powder with half a pint of water, and put this with the almonds into a stewpan, with a chicken cut up into joints
5. Let it simmer gently for nearly an hour
6. Add a teacup-ful of cream and let it nearly boil
7. Squeeze into it the juice of a lemon, and serve up

title: Mrs. W.'s Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- one rather large onion
- one fowl
- two or three apples
- water or gravy
- one tablespoonful of currie powder
- one dessert-spoonful of dry mustard
- milk
- half a tea-cupful of vinegar
- one lemon
- salt

## Directions

1. Take a piece of butter twice as big as a walnut, fry it with a rather large onion cut into thin slices
2. Cut up a fowl into joints, and put it into a stewpan with a fried onion
3. Simmer it for a quarter of an hour
4. Add two or three chopped apples, and as much water or gravy as will cover it
5. Let it stew slowly for an hour
6. Take a tablespoonful of currie powder, and a dessert-spoonful of dry mustard, mix them together with milk into a liquid, and pour it into the stewpan
7. Add half a tea-cupful of vinegar, the juice of a lemon, and a little salt
8. Simmer it for half an hour, skim it well, and then serve it up

title: Mulligatawny
body:
## Ingredients

- one fine fowl
- two quarts of cold water
- two chickens
- two tablespoonfuls of currie powder
- one sliced onion
- a clove of garlic
- a little salt
- the juice of a lime or small lemon
- a piece of butter mixed with a little flour
- a wineglassful of Madeira
- a little fried onion

## Directions

1. Cut up the fowl and put it into a pan with two quarts of cold water
2. Let it simmer until all the juice of the meat is extracted
3. Strain it, and when cold, put to the liquor two chickens, cut as for a fricassee
4. Add two tablespoonfuls of currie powder, one sliced onion and a clove of garlic, a little salt, the juice of a lime or small lemon, a piece of butter mixed with a little flour, and a wineglassful of Madeira
5. When ready to dish up, which will be in about three-quarters of an hour, throw in a little fried onion
6. Serve rice with it, but in another dish

title: Mulligatawny Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit or fowl
- five fried onions
- a small piece of garlic
- three pints of stock
- two tablespoonfuls of currie powder
- one ounce of pounded almonds
- a little lemon or mango juice
- a good lump of butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Skin and cut up a rabbit or fowl into small joints
2. Brown them in a frying-pan
3. Put them into a stewpan with five fried onions, a small piece of garlic, and three pints of stock into which you have previously mixed two tablespoonfuls of currie powder
4. Simmer gently for two hours
5. Add one ounce of pounded almonds and mix well
6. Add a little lemon or mango juice, with a good lump of butter and salt to taste
7. Serve very hot and send up a large dish of boiled rice with it, or the rice may be put into the soup, if preferred

title: Orange Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- four oranges
- quarter of a pound butter
- grated orange peel
- bread crumbs
- yolks of four eggs

## Directions

1. Peel and quarter four oranges, take out the pips
2. Stew them until quite soft
3. Beat them in a basin with a quarter of a pound of butter, a little grated orange peel, a few bread crumbs, and the well beaten yolks of four eggs
4. Sweeten to taste
5. Pour it into a pie dish
6. Bake in a gentle oven

title: Our Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- one moderate sized fowl
- one sliced onion
- one onion fried brown
- one clove of garlic
- one teaspoonful of salt
- one tablespoonful and a half of currie powder
- one lemon or one tablespoonful of vinegar
- a small piece of mint
- water or gravy
- a quarter of a pound of butter

## Directions

1. Skin a moderate sized fowl, and cut it up
2. Put it into a stewpan with one sliced onion, and another previously fried brown, one clove of garlic, one teaspoonful of salt, one tablespoonful and a half of currie powder, and the juice of one lemon, or one tablespoonful of vinegar
3. Add a small piece of mint
4. Cover the meat with water or gravy, and let it stew very slowly for an hour and a half
5. Add a quarter of a pound of butter, and let it stew an hour and a half more

title: Pillau
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of rice
- half a pound of butter
- salt
- peppercorns
- cloves
- mace
- a pound and a half of bacon
- two fowls
- hard boiled eggs
- fried whole onions

## Directions

1. Wash two pounds of rice, boil it in a little water with half a pound of butter, some salt, peppercorns, cloves, and mace
2. Keep the saucepan closely covered until the rice is sufficiently cooked
3. Have ready a pound and a half of bacon, and two fowls nicely boiled
4. Place the bacon in the middle of a dish, and the fowls on each side
5. Cover over with the boiled rice, and garnish with hard boiled eggs and fried whole onions

title: Pish Pash
body:
## Ingredients

- half a fowl
- about a quart of water
- the other half fowl
- half a teacupful of rice
- a blade or two of mace
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Put half a fowl into a saucepan with about a quart of water, let it boil to rags
2. strain off the meat, and to the liquor add the other half fowl cut up into joints, half a teacupful of rice, a blade or two of mace, and pepper and salt to taste
3. Let this stew until the fowl is very tender and nearly all the gravy is absorbed
4. send to table

title: Pish Pash
body:
## Ingredients

- half a fowl
- water
- the other half fowl
- half a teacupful of rice
- a blade or two of mace
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Put half a fowl into a saucepan with about a quart of water, let it boil to rags, then strain off the meat
2. To the liquor add the other half fowl cut up into joints, half a teacupful of rice, a blade or two of mace, and pepper and salt to taste
3. Let this stew until the fowl is very tender and nearly all the gravy is absorbed, then send to table

title: Prawn Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoonful of currie powder
- one teaspoonful of flour
- water
- half a pint of good stock or gravy soup
- a large onion
- two dozen large prawns
- two ounces of butter
- tablespoonful of vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix one tablespoonful of currie powder and one teaspoonful of flour, with a little water, into a smooth paste
2. Stir it into half a pint of good stock or gravy soup
3. Add a large onion, sliced and fried, and simmer it in a stewpan until it thickens
4. Have ready two dozen large prawns, taken whole from their shells, and put them into the stewpan
5. Simmer for a quarter of an hour, stirring occasionally, care being taken not to break the prawns
6. Then add two ounces of butter, and a tablespoonful of vinegar, and simmer for a quarter of an hour longer

title: Preserved Cucumbers or Melons
body:
## Ingredients

- large cucumbers or melons (green)
- strong salt and water
- vine leaves
- alum and water
- cold spring water
- one pound of loaf sugar
- half an ounce of powdered ginger
- water
- the juice and rind of a lemon
- rum

## Directions

1. Take large cucumbers or melons, such as are green, put them into a jar of strong salt and water, with vine leaves on the top
2. Set them by the fireside until they turn yellow, then wash them
3. Put them on a slow fire in alum and water, covered with vine leaves
4. Let them boil till they become green, take them off and leave them in the liquor until they are cold
5. Quarter them and take out the soft part and seeds, if there are any
6. Put them in cold spring water, changing it twice a day for three days
7. Have ready a syrup made as follows: To one pound of loaf sugar add half an ounce of powdered ginger, and as much water as will moisten it
8. Boil it and skim it well
9. When it is quite free from scum and boiling fast, add the juice and rind of a lemon
10. When this syrup is quite cold pour it over the cucumber or melon
11. Should the syrup be too thin after standing two or three days, boil it again with a little more sugar
12. A spoonful of rum gives it the West Indian flavour

title: Preserved Peaches
body:
## Ingredients

- strong syrup
- fine peaches
- good brandy

## Directions

1. Make a strong syrup, which boil and skim well
2. Have ready some fine peaches, gathered before they are over ripe
3. Rub the lint off them carefully with a cloth
4. Take a needle and run it down the seam of each peach to the depth of the skin, and place them in a jar, covering them with good brandy, and let them remain two hours
5. Take them out and put them into a preserving pan, so that each may lie singly
6. Pour the syrup over them, and boil them until they look clear, but be careful not to break them
7. Remove the peaches with a spoon into glasses, and when the syrup is cold, mix it with the brandy and pour it on the peaches
8. The glasses must be well tied down with a bladder, and the air carefully excluded

title: Preserved Pine
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound white sugar
- quarter of a pint water
- pine apple

## Directions

1. Make a syrup with one pound of white sugar to a quarter of a pint of water
2. Boil it until it becomes clear, skimming it carefully
3. Have ready a pine apple peeled and cut into slices
4. When the syrup is ready and boiling, put in the slices of pine, and simmer for about three quarters of an hour
5. Put into glasses or jars, and when cold tie down

title: The Epicure's Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- one capsicum
- two eschalots
- one or two bird's-eye chilies
- two tablespoonfuls of port wine
- six tablespoonfuls of mushroom catsup
- two tablespoonfuls of soy
- two tablespoonfuls of walnut catsup
- half a teaspoonful of cayenne
- the same quantity of white pepper
- half a pint of vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop fine, one capsicum, two eschalots, and one or two bird's-eye chilies
2. add two tablespoonfuls of port wine, six tablespoonfuls of mushroom catsup, two of soy, and two of walnut catsup, half a teaspoonful of cayenne, and the same quantity of white pepper
3. put all into a bottle, which place in a warm place for two or three weeks
4. then strain, and add half a pint of vinegar

title: Tippoo's Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- the juice of four lemons
- a few pieces of lemon peel
- a small quantity of salt
- a little tamarind juice
- half a teaspoonful of Cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Put into a saucepan the juice of four lemons, a few pieces of lemon peel, a small quantity of salt, a little tamarind juice, and half a teaspoonful of Cayenne pepper
2. simmer this for a few minutes, and turn it into a basin
3. Strain through a fine strainer when cold, and then bottle it into small bottles, which should be well corked, and kept in a place free from

title: To Boil Rice for Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the rice in several waters, then leave it in a basin of cold water to soak for two or three hours
2. Have ready a saucepan full of water, with a little salt in it
3. When the water boils, drain the rice and put it into the saucepan
4. Let it boil very quickly for about seven minutes, then pour it into a colander, and place the colander on the top of the saucepan, that the water may quite drain off
5. The rice ought to be stirred with a fork that the grains may be separated
6. It ought to be boiled in a large quantity of water, and it will be sufficiently cooked when the grains become a little soft, and over done if they at all stick together
7. Rice should always be served in a separate dish from the currie

title: Vegetable Currie
body:
## Ingredients

- four large potatoes
- butter
- pint of brown gravy
- one raw onion
- one previously fried onion
- half a small vegetable marrow
- a handful of green peas
- a handful of French beans
- a few slices of cucumber
- one tablespoonful and a half of currie powder
- tablespoonful of vinegar
- salt
- a quarter of a pound of butter
- teaspoonful of flour
- a small piece of mint

## Directions

1. Peel and cut up in square pieces, four large potatoes, and fry them in butter until they are a light brown colour
2. Put them into a stewpan with a pint of brown gravy, one raw onion, and one previously fried, half a small vegetable marrow cut into pieces, a handful of green peas, the same of French beans, and a few slices of cucumber
3. Add one tablespoonful and a half of currie powder, a tablespoonful of vinegar, and salt to taste
4. Simmer very slowly, stirring carefully from time to time until the vegetables are nearly cooked
5. Then add a quarter of a pound of butter mixed with a teaspoonful of flour to thicken the gravy, and simmer again until the vegetables are sufficiently cooked but not broken
6. A small piece of mint is by some considered an improvement

title: Vegetable Mulligatawny
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of butter
- two or three small vegetable marrows
- two large onions
- three or four acid apples
- two or three tomatos
- a cucumber
- a tablespoonful and a half or two tablespoonfuls of currie powder
- salt
- good stock
- a squeeze of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Put a quarter of a pound of butter into a frying pan and slightly brown it
2. Pour it into a large saucepan
3. Add two or three small vegetable marrows cut up and freed from seeds, two large onions, three or four acid apples peeled and cored, two or three tomatos if in season, and a cucumber cut up, taking care not to put any of the seeds in
4. Stew these gently until tender
5. Add a tablespoonful and a half or two tablespoonfuls of currie powder, and salt to taste
6. Let this simmer about a quarter of an hour longer, adding sufficient good stock to cover it
7. Reduce it all to a pulp and press it through a sieve
8. Put the liquid into a fresh saucepan with as much fresh stock as will make it the required thinness
9. Add a squeeze of lemon juice if liked
10. Serve the soup as hot as possible

title: Yam Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- moderate sized yam
- half a pound butter
- half a pound sugar
- cinnamon
- eight eggs
- orange flower water

## Directions

1. Take a moderate sized yam, and boil it until it is soft
2. Peel it and beat it fine with half a pound of butter, half a pound of sugar, and a little cinnamon
3. Beat up eight eggs, leaving out four whites, with a little orange flower water
4. Put the mixture into a buttered dish
5. Bake it
